feedback.text: Please consider stopping companies from leaving videogames in an unplayable state after support for them ends. A well known instance of this phenomena is The Crew videogame. After support for it ended from the publisher, not only it stopped working completely for anyone who owned that game, but Ubisoft removed the game from consumers' libraries who paid for the game, so they didn't even have access to the game's files to even be able to attempt to fix the game to work without official servers. What is this if not destruction of digital goods?
